Always preceded by "chi̍t". Signifies an indefinite, small quantity. The noun modified by "kóa" does not need a measure word; "kóa" itself is a measure word.
"bô kúi + Measure" means not many/only a few.
Usually used with the first person; not used with negatives or in past tense.
